organic-tell-me-how-much-you-pay-to-send.hook: Tell me how much you pay to send $200 home — I'll show you what it should cost 👇

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-8s): Creator faces camera, asks viewers to drop their country + fee in comments, shows 3 real comment examples on screen. Beat 2 (9-25s): Screen-record of Speed app sending $200 — fee line reads $0.00, arrival time reads 'instant'. Beat 3 (26-45s): Text overlay compares Western Union ($12.99), MoneyGram ($9.99), Speed ($0) side by side. Beat 4 (46-58s): Creator says 'Drop your country below — I'm making part 2 with the worst fees I find.'
payload.production_notes: Film vertically on phone; use TikTok's green-screen effect to show app screen behind face during beat 2; no music needed — silence on fee reveal is the punchline

organic-i-sent-bitcoin-to-my-mom-in-4-se.hook: I sent Bitcoin to my mom in 4 seconds and she didn't need a wallet, a bank, or any crypto knowledge

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-7s): Creator holds phone to camera — 'Watch me do this live, no cuts.' Beat 2 (8-28s): Raw screen-record: open Speed, type amount, hit send, notification pops on second phone held up to camera. Beat 3 (29-45s): Creator zooms in on timestamp — '4 seconds'. Says 'She received pesos, I sent sats, no fees.' Beat 4 (46-58s): 'If you've never used Bitcoin, this is the one thing worth trying — link in bio.'
payload.production_notes: Two phones needed — one recording, one receiving; keep it one-take to reinforce authenticity; avoid crypto jargon beyond 'sats'; caption: 'POV: your first Bitcoin transaction takes 4 seconds'

organic-the-us-sent-150-billion-in-remit.hook: The US sent $150 billion in remittances last year. Banks kept $8 billion of it in fees.

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-6s): Stark text-on-screen: '$8,000,000,000 taken from immigrant families every year.' Beat 2 (7-22s): Creator voiceover — 'That's money meant for rent, food, school — gone to middlemen.' Shows stock-style footage of phone transfer screens with fee lines highlighted. Beat 3 (23-42s): 'Bitcoin fixes this. Lightning Network = instant, zero fee, no bank.' Speed app logo appears. Beat 4 (43-58s): 'Download Speed Wallet. Keep 100% of what you send.'
payload.production_notes: Use CapCut text-animation template for beat 1; royalty-free lo-fi beat underneath; creator does not need to appear on camera — voiceover only keeps production at $0; post Tuesday 6–8pm for peak Latin American diaspora scroll time

organic-reply-to-this-what-does-the-ligh.hook: Reply to this: what does the Lightning Network actually do? (I'll keep it under 60 seconds)

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-5s): Show a real comment asking 'what is Lightning Network?' pinned on screen. Beat 2 (6-25s): Simple animation drawn on whiteboard or iPad: Bitcoin blockchain = slow highway, Lightning = express lane on top. Beat 3 (26-45s): Screen-record Speed app — send $10 internationally, show 3-second confirmation, $0 fee. Beat 4 (46-58s): 'That express lane is what Speed runs on. Try your first send free — link in bio.'
payload.production_notes: Reply-to-comment format proven by hook index 12; use iPad + Apple Pencil or physical whiteboard for beat 2 — no design software needed; seed the 'question comment' yourself if needed to trigger the format

organic-did-you-know-nepal-s-entire-econ.hook: Did you know Nepal's entire economy depends on money sent from abroad — and banks take up to 7% of every transfer?

payload.outline: Beat 1 (0-8s): Creator on camera, personal tone — 'My [family member/friend] sends money home every month. I just found out how much disappears.' Beat 2 (9-28s): Graphic: $500 sent → $465 received (7% fee). Then Speed: $500 sent → $500 received. Beat 3 (29-45s): 'Lightning payments mean the full amount lands instantly. No correspondent banks, no cut.' Beat 4 (46-58s): 'If you send money home, try one transfer on Speed this week — zero fees, I promise.'
payload.production_notes: Inspired by hook index 15's Nepal backbone angle; creator should be from or connected to a remittance-heavy community for authenticity; film in natural light at home; add subtitles — 85% of Reels watched muted; target posting to coincide with typical Friday payday send cycle

paid-you-just-lost-14-you-didn-t-even-no.hook: You just lost $14. You didn't even notice.
paid-you-just-lost-14-you-didn-t-even-no.estimate: $1.80-$2.60 — fear-of-loss fee contrast targets high-intent remittance senders already in purchase mindset; below $3.17 benchmark due to emotional specificity and zero-jargon creative

payload.problem: Cut to split-screen: left side shows a Western Union receipt ($14.99 fee on a $200 send), right side shows a family abroad receiving less than expected. Voiceover: 'Every time you send money home, banks quietly take a cut. On a $200 send, that's up to $15 gone — every single month, that's $180 a year stolen from your family.'
payload.solution: Screen-record of Speed app: type $200, hit send, fee shows $0.00, confirmation in 3 seconds. Voiceover: 'Speed Wallet runs on Bitcoin Lightning — instant transfers, zero fees, globally. Your family gets every dollar you send.'
payload.cta: Download Speed. Send your first $200 free. Zero fees, always.
payload.audience: Meta (Facebook + Instagram): ages 22-45, Hispanic/Latino US, Filipino-American, South Asian-American, interests: international money transfer, Western Union, Remitly, family support, wire transfers. Exclude: existing crypto wallets

paid-i-bought-bitcoin-for-the-first-time.hook: I bought Bitcoin for the first time in 47 seconds.
paid-i-bought-bitcoin-for-the-first-time.estimate: $2.40-$3.10 — curiosity-gap hook with aspirational speed claim matches TikTok's impulsive install behavior; near benchmark but high volume potential from broad crypto-curious pool

payload.problem: Talking-head UGC style: creator on phone, casual setting — 'I kept hearing about Bitcoin but every app looked like it was built for Wall Street. Confusing charts, weird wallets, verification that took days. I gave up three times.' Text overlay: '63% of Americans are curious about crypto but have never tried it.'
payload.solution: Raw screen-record of Speed onboarding: download → account created → first sat purchased → sent to a friend, all under 60 seconds. Voiceover: 'Speed is the first Bitcoin app that actually makes sense. Buy, send, receive — that's it. No charts, no jargon, no waiting.' End on confirmation screen with timestamp visible.
payload.cta: Download Speed Wallet. Your first Bitcoin in under a minute.
payload.audience: TikTok: ages 18-34, interests: personal finance, fintech apps, Robinhood, Cash App, Venmo users, crypto-curious but not yet buyers. Lookalike: Cash App installs. Exclude: Bitcoin/crypto wallet owners

paid-your-casino-payout-is-sitting-there.hook: Your casino payout is sitting there. Why can't you withdraw it yet?
paid-your-casino-payout-is-sitting-there.estimate: $2.90-$4.20 — iGaming audience is high-intent but smaller and more competitive; upper range may exceed benchmark in regulated-state geo-targeting but LTV of iGaming user justifies acquisition cost; optimize toward withdrawal-intent audiences to compress toward lower bound

payload.problem: Fast cuts of frustrated UX: spinning withdrawal screens, '3-5 business days' notifications, support chat bubbles with no reply. Voiceover: 'Traditional payment rails mean iGaming platforms hold your winnings hostage. ACH delays. Card declines. Limits. You won — but you can't touch it.'
payload.solution: Side-by-side demo: legacy withdrawal (pending, day 3 counter ticking up) vs Speed Lightning withdrawal (amount confirmed, 'Received' in 2 seconds). Voiceover: 'Speed Wallet connects to Lightning-enabled gaming platforms. Withdraw instantly, any amount, any time — it's your money.' Show Speed logo + compatible platform logos.
payload.cta: Get Speed Wallet. Instant crypto withdrawals, zero wait.
payload.audience: Meta + TikTok: ages 21-45, male skew 70%, interests: online poker, sports betting, DraftKings, FanDuel, BetMGM, online casino, cryptocurrency payments. Geo: US states where iGaming is legal (NJ, PA, MI, WV, CT, DE). Retarget: visited iGaming platform sites in last 30 days
